title: Hepetis vallisoletana
intro: Hepetis vallisoletana, also known as Valerian of Valladolid, is a perennial herb belonging to the Valerianaceae family. The plant is valued for its sedative and relaxing properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The bioactive constituents of Hepetis vallisoletana include valerenic acid, valeranone, and valeranone derivatives, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to promote sleep and relaxation.
taxonomy: Hepetis vallisoletana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant belongs to the genus Helictotrichon, which is a group of grasses in the family Poaceae, but is actually a synonym of a different plant.
